Name: Shoot For The Stars Aim For The Moon [Promo Art]
Notes (Join the Discord if you have any: This artwork was posted by Pop Smoke’s label “Victor Victor” on the 14th of May 2020 to announce the album’s release date. A lot of people thought this was going to be the album cover, but the label later confirmed on twitter that this was not the case. The album was initially intended to release June 12th, 2020, however due to protests over the death of George Floyd around this time, it was decided that the album was to be pushed back to July 3rd, 2020 out of respect, with the single "Make It Rain" releasing June 12th on its own.

Notes (Join the Discord if you have any: Cover art for French Montana's remix of Lil Tjay's "Zoo York", already featuring Fivio Foreign and Pop Smoke.

Name: Shoot For The Stars Aim For The Moon [Virgil Abloh Version]
Notes (Join the Discord if you have any: This was meant to be the original artwork for Pop Smoke’s posthumous debut album. It was designed by Louis Vuitton artistic director and Off-White founder Virgil Abloh. After it was revealed a few days before the album’s release, it was strongly criticized by fans for being too simple and of low quality. Due to this feedback, Pop Smoke’s former manager and label head Steven Victor, announced that they will change the artwork shortly after. Later, it was replaced with a silver rose on a black square, the actual artwork we can see on the album now.
